name: Net Average and Real Incremental Rates of Profit

book_quotes.quote: Combining the two gives us the net rates of profit displayed in figure 16.8. We now see two things: first, that the Stagflation Crisis of the late 1960s was precipitated when both rates sank to unprecedented lows. The whole behavior of the system changed at this point: growth slowed, bankruptcies and business failures soared, unemployment rose sharply, real wages fell relative to productivity, and the stock market fell by over 56% in real terms - about the same as it did in the worst part of the Great Depression.

Figure 16.8 Net Average and Real Incremental Rates of Profit, US Corporations, 1947-2011. Average and current (real) incremental rates of profit from Appendix 6.8.II.7, minus the 3-month T-bill rate in Appendix 16.2 Data Tables for Chapter 16, sheet = Ch 16 Data.

primary_source.agency: BEA NIPA + Fixed Assets (profit rate inputs) and US Treasury / FRB (3-month T-bill)
primary_source.publication: Shaikh Appendix 6.8.II.7 (corporate profit rates) minus 3-month T-bill from ERP Table 73
primary_source.table_or_series_id: Composite: rcorp + iroprcorp (Appendix 6.8.II.7) minus i_3mo (TB3MS annual)
primary_source.available_through: BEA NIPA T1.14, T6.16D (corporate profits); BEA Fixed Assets T6.1, T6.3 (capital stock); FRED TB3MS

formula: r_net = r_corp - i_3mo  AND  rI_HP100_net(-1) = HP100(iroprcorp)(-1) - i_3mo
components: rcorp (US corporate average rate of profit, after-tax/Profit-share-adjusted) from Shaikh Appendix 6.8.II.7
iroprcorp = real corporate incremental rate of profit from Appendix 6.8.II.7
HP100-smoothed iroprcorp, lagged one period (rI HP100(-1))
3-month US Treasury bill rate (annual avg, FRED TB3MS / ERP Table 73 col 1)

extension_candidates.source: BEA NIPA Table 1.14 (Gross Value Added of Corporate Business) / BEA Fixed Assets Table 6.1 (Net stock, corporate)
concerns: BEA periodic comprehensive revisions to NIPA and Fixed Assets; profit-share definitions sensitive to CCAdj/IVA treatment
Shaikh's exact rcorp formula needs reproduction from Appendix 6.8 sources

extension_candidates.subseries_label: Alternative: net incremental rate via discrete-Hodrick-Prescott filter
extension_candidates.source: Replicated from BEA inputs via S0612/S0617 in RSCD chain
concerns: HP filter lambda=100 (annual) endpoints shift on each extension; document as known fragility

methodology_notes: Series is a difference: profit rate minus 3-month T-bill rate. The two underlying components are S0608 (rcorp from Appendix 6.8.II.7, the chapter-6 profit rate dataset) and the US 3-month T-bill (Fig 16.6 series).

Per CD2 (S099), five subseries: S099-A (rcorp), S099-B (iroprcorp), S099-C (HP-filtered IROP), S099-D (HP-filtered IROP lagged 1), S099-E (rcorp adjusted for wage suppression, rcorpalt = rcorp*(Pcorpalt/Pcorp)).
The wage-suppression-adjusted variant rcorpalt directly leverages the counterfactual profit path constructed in S1604 (Fig 16.4) — internal cross-reference.
Network of dependencies: Fig 16.8 = Fig 16.5 (gross rates) - Fig 16.6 (T-bill), so any revision to chapter-6 profit rates (S0606-S0608) cascades here.
open_questions: Confirm CD2 split between S099 dossier (this) and overlap with chapter 6 profit-rate dossiers (S0606-S0608) — avoid double-counting in cross-series uniqueness check.
HP-filter lambda for annual series: Shaikh uses lambda=100; some literature recommends lambda=6.25 for annual. Document choice in Phase 5.
rcorpalt construction requires replicating Fig 16.4 counterfactual; spec needed for Phase 5.

review_history.notes: Ported from CD2 S099; verbatim quotes from p. 734 narrative and Appendix 16.1 p. 899.
